labels.analysis: The MCP Server "Math MCP Learning" provides 17 tools focused on data analysis & processing tasks. Core capabilities include analysis and computation, creation and generation, data access and reading. Key tools: calculate, statistics, compound_interest, convert_units, matrix_multiply.
labels.reasoning: Primary label "Data Analysis & Processing" assigned based on strong keyword matches in tool names and descriptions: data, chart, statistics.
labels.custom_label: Data Analysis & Processing - Math MCP Learning

tools.name: calculate
tools.title: Mathematical Calculator
tools.description:
Safely evaluate mathematical expressions with support for basic operations and math functions.

Supported operations: +, -, *, /, **, ()
Supported functions: sin, cos, tan, log, sqrt, abs, pow

Examples:
- "2 + 3 * 4" → 14
- "sqrt(16)" → 4.0
- "sin(3.14159/2)" → 1.0

outputSchema.description: Result of a mathematical expression evaluation.

tools.name: statistics
tools.title: Statistical Analysis
tools.description:
Perform statistical calculations on a list of numbers.

Available operations: mean, median, mode, std_dev, variance

tools.name: compound_interest
tools.description:
Calculate compound interest for investments.

Formula: A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt)
Where:
- P = principal amount
- r = annual interest rate (as decimal)
- n = number of times interest compounds per year
- t = time in years

tools.name: convert_units
tools.description:
Convert between different units of measurement.

Supported unit types:
- length: mm, cm, m, km, in, ft, yd, mi
- weight: g, kg, oz, lb
- temperature: c, f, k (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin)

tools.name: matrix_multiply
tools.title: Matrix Multiplication
tools.description:
Multiply two matrices (A × B).

Args:
    matrix_a: First matrix (m x n)
    matrix_b: Second matrix (n x p)

Returns:
    Result matrix (m x p)

Examples:
    matrix_multiply([[1, 2], [3, 4]], [[5, 6], [7, 8]])
    matrix_multiply([[1, 2, 3]], [[1], [2], [3]])

tools.name: matrix_transpose
tools.title: Matrix Transpose
tools.description:
Transpose a matrix (swap rows and columns).

Args:
    matrix: Input matrix (m x n)

Returns:
    Transposed matrix (n x m)

Examples:
    matrix_transpose([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]])
    matrix_transpose([[1], [2], [3]])

tools.name: matrix_determinant
tools.title: Matrix Determinant
tools.description:
Calculate the determinant of a square matrix.

Args:
    matrix: Square matrix (n x n)

Returns:
    Determinant value (scalar)

Examples:
    matrix_determinant([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
    matrix_determinant([[1, 0, 0], [0, 1, 0], [0, 0, 1]])  # Identity matrix

tools.name: matrix_inverse
tools.title: Matrix Inverse
tools.description:
Calculate the inverse of a square matrix.

Args:
    matrix: Square matrix (n x n)

Returns:
    Inverse matrix (n x n)

Examples:
    matrix_inverse([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
    matrix_inverse([[2, 0], [0, 2]])  # Diagonal matrix

tools.name: matrix_eigenvalues
tools.title: Matrix Eigenvalues
tools.description:
Calculate the eigenvalues of a square matrix.

Args:
    matrix: Square matrix (n x n)

Returns:
    List of eigenvalues (may be complex numbers)

Examples:
    matrix_eigenvalues([[4, 2], [1, 3]])
    matrix_eigenvalues([[3, 0, 0], [0, 5, 0], [0, 0, 7]])  # Diagonal matrix

tools.name: save_calculation
tools.title: Save Calculation to Workspace
tools.description:
Save calculation to persistent workspace (survives restarts).

Args:
    name: Variable name to save under
    expression: The mathematical expression
    result: The calculated result

Examples:
    save_calculation("portfolio_return", "10000 * 1.07^5", 14025.52)
    save_calculation("circle_area", "pi * 5^2", 78.54)

outputSchema.description: Result of saving a calculation to the workspace.

tools.name: load_variable
tools.description:
Load previously saved calculation result from workspace.

Args:
    name: Variable name to load

Examples:
    load_variable("portfolio_return")  # Returns saved calculation
    load_variable("circle_area")       # Access across sessions

outputSchema.description: Result of loading a variable from the workspace.

tools.name: plot_function
tools.title: Function Plotter
tools.description:
Generate mathematical function plots (requires matplotlib).

Args:
    expression: Mathematical expression to plot (e.g., "x**2", "sin(x)")
    x_range: Tuple of (min, max) for x-axis range
    num_points: Number of points to plot (default: 100)
    ctx: FastMCP context for logging

Returns:
    Image object with PNG data or VisualizationError on failure

Examples:
    plot_function("x**2", (-5, 5))
    plot_function("sin(x)", (-3.14, 3.14))

tools.name: create_histogram
tools.title: Statistical Histogram
tools.description:
Create statistical histograms (requires matplotlib).

Args:
    data: List of numerical values
    bins: Number of histogram bins (default: 20)
    title: Chart title
    ctx: FastMCP context for logging

Returns:
    Dict with base64-encoded PNG image or error message

Examples:
    create_histogram([1.0, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 5.0])
    create_histogram([10, 20, 30, 40, 50], bins=5, title="Test Scores")

tools.name: plot_line_chart
tools.title: Line Chart
tools.description:
Create a line chart from data points (requires matplotlib).

Args:
    x_data: X-axis data points
    y_data: Y-axis data points
    title: Chart title
    x_label: X-axis label
    y_label: Y-axis label
    color: Line color (name or hex code, e.g., 'blue', '#2E86AB')
    show_grid: Whether to show grid lines
    ctx: FastMCP context for logging

Returns:
    Dict with base64-encoded PNG image or error message

Examples:
    plot_line_chart([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 4, 9, 16], title="Squares")
    plot_line_chart([0, 1, 2], [0, 1, 4], color='red', x_label='Time', y_label='Distance')

tools.name: plot_scatter_chart
tools.title: Scatter Plot
tools.description:
Create a scatter plot from data points (requires matplotlib).

Args:
    x_data: X-axis data points
    y_data: Y-axis data points
    title: Chart title
    x_label: X-axis label
    y_label: Y-axis label
    color: Point color (name or hex code, e.g., 'blue', '#2E86AB')
    point_size: Size of scatter points (default: 50)
    ctx: FastMCP context for logging

Returns:
    Dict with base64-encoded PNG image or error message

Examples:
    plot_scatter_chart([1, 2, 3, 4], [1, 4, 9, 16], title="Correlation Study")
    plot_scatter_chart([1, 2, 3], [2, 4, 5], color='purple', point_size=100)

tools.name: plot_box_plot
tools.title: Box Plot
tools.description:
Create a box plot for comparing distributions (requires matplotlib).

Args:
    data_groups: List of data groups to compare
    group_labels: Optional labels for each group
    title: Chart title
    y_label: Y-axis label
    color: Box color (name or hex code, e.g., 'blue', '#2E86AB')
    ctx: FastMCP context for logging

Returns:
    Dict with base64-encoded PNG image or error message

Examples:
    plot_box_plot([[1, 2, 3, 4, 5], [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]], group_labels=["A", "B"])
    plot_box_plot([[10, 20, 30], [15, 25, 35], [5, 15, 25]], title="Comparison")

tools.name: plot_financial_line
tools.title: Financial Line Chart
tools.description:
Generate and plot synthetic financial price data (requires matplotlib).

Creates realistic price movement patterns for educational purposes.
Does not use real market data.

Args:
    days: Number of days to generate (default: 30)
    trend: Market trend ('bullish', 'bearish', or 'volatile')
    start_price: Starting price value (default: 100.0)
    color: Line color (name or hex code, e.g., 'blue', '#2E86AB')
    ctx: FastMCP context for logging

Returns:
    Dict with base64-encoded PNG image or error message

Examples:
    plot_financial_line(days=60, trend='bullish')
    plot_financial_line(days=90, trend='volatile', start_price=150.0, color='orange')
